danger_signals.visual_form: Radar screen display (bottom center of cockpit HUD)
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile contact identified; immediate threat to player aircraft

danger_signals.visual_form: AIM9M missile targeting reticle on HUD
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy has achieved weapons lock; imminent attack danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Cockpit instrument degradation and warning displays
danger_signals.meaning: Aircraft structural integrity compromised; increasing risk of loss of control
Risk of collision with ground; navigational hazard in mountainous terrain

safety_signals.visual_form: Radar screen identification (IFF - Identification Friend or Foe)
safety_signals.meaning: Allied wingman or support aircraft in vicinity; reduced isolation risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Radar screen with no hostile contacts
safety_signals.meaning: No immediate aerial threats; safe to conduct patrol or transit

safety_signals.visual_form: Fuel gauge and weapons inventory display in cockpit
safety_signals.meaning: Sustained operational capability; can continue mission

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Base locations in Iraq, Israel, Panama, Nevada (Red Flag exercise)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct aircraft silhouettes on radar and in 3D viewport
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Iraqi territorial airspace; campaign scenario theater
Israeli airspace and terrain in campaign scenarios

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: MiG-29: Deadly Adversary expansion features this as named opponent
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Future conflict scenario in Operation Fighting Tiger expansion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dynamic mission briefing with objective markers; terrain specific to theater (Iraqi desert, Israeli hills, Panamanian jungle)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI wingman support; enemy patrol patterns; supply replenishment cycles affecting available aircraft and weapons

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Red Flag training exercise in Nevada with controlled airspace; clear weather; marked training zones
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Structured dogfighting scenarios; tutorial guidance; predictable enemy behavior patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Roster reduction in campaign screen; fewer available aircraft for subsequent missions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Limited pilot pool means mission availability decreases; forces strategic mission selection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Weapon loadout menu shows limited ammunition and ordnance availability
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Periodic replenishment events; forces players to manage loadout carefully across multiple missions

environmental_storytelling.example: Mountains, hills, valleys with unique shapes in Iraq, Israel, Panama create geopolitical theater identity through terrain; players recognize region through landforms
Limited supplies of pilots, aircraft, and weapons that periodically replenish communicate ongoing conflict sustainability; attrition tells story of prolonged engagement
Campaign scenarios set in real geopolitical hotspots (Iraq, Israel, Panama) communicate international military tension and regional conflict narrative
Operation Fighting Tiger introduces Japan vs. Russia future conflict scenario; geographic expansion of narrative scope signals escalation of world-state complexity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Active radar returns; evasive maneuvers; weapons launch indicators visible on HUD and radar
Maintains formation; returns positive IFF on radar; can follow player directives
Stationary on terrain; appear as radar blips only when in range; destructible through ordnance
Cockpit instruments degrade; flight model responsiveness decreases; visual damage indicators in instrumentation

notes: Falcon 3.0 communicates world-state primarily through high-fidelity cockpit simulation and radar interface rather than traditional narrative cinematics. The Manifestation Layer relies on instrumental readouts, terrain visualization, and mission parameters rather than dynamic environmental changes. The campaign engine's logistics system (pilot/aircraft/weapons replenishment) creates emergent narrative through resource scarcity. Expansion packs extend manifestation through geographic and temporal scenario variations (Japan-Russia future conflict, additional naval strike scenarios). The game's 'padlock' view mechanic allows player perspective to follow targets dynamically, manifesting tactical focus and threat prioritization as a camera behavior.
